text: Endurance exercise stimulates oxidation of amino acids, which may depress rates of skeletal muscle protein synthesis and negatively affect training adaptation.
Amino acid contribution to total energy expenditure during endurance exercise is relatively low compared to carbohydrates and fat.
A low supply of amino acids relative to carbohydrates may negatively affect synthesis of capillaries, mitochondrial protein turnover, and oxygen transport proteins including hemoglobin and myoglobin.
The role of dietary protein in endurance training adaptations has been historically underappreciated due to the paradigm that endurance training has insignificant effects on skeletal muscle growth.
Evidence for the role of protein on endurance training adaptations and performance remains scarce and is mainly derived from acute exercise studies rather than chronic training interventions.
